perturbation.original_text: AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, that is at least equal to the lower of (x) AIG's then-current rating from such agency or (y) the Company's then-current rating as supported by this Agreement from such agency; or (ii) such that, immediately on the effective date of the sale by AIG of the Company, the Company's capitalization is consistent with the minimum capital adequacy standards and criteria of at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, for a rating that is equal to or better than the Company's then-current rating on the date immediately preceding such sale.
perturbation.changed_text: AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, that is at least equal to the lower of (x) AIG's then-current rating from such agency or (y) the Company's then-current rating as supported by this Agreement from such agency; or (ii) such that, immediately on the effective date of the sale by AIG of the Company, the Company's capitalization is consistent with the minimum capital adequacy standards and criteria of at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, for a rating that is equal to or better than the Company's then-current rating on the date immediately preceding such sale.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notice period for termination from 30 days to 10 days could violate Delaware law, which requires a 30-day notice for contract terminations under certain circumstances.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Delaware Code Title 6, В§ 2-309. Absence of specific time provisions; notice of termination.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law requires reasonable notification to terminate an ongoing contract, and reducing the period to 10 days for an agreement concerning capital maintenance for an insurance company may not be considered reasonable, especially if it disrupts the company's operations or capital planning. See 6 Del. C. В§ 2-309(3).

Laws, c. 349. ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 2-302. Unconscionable contract or clause. (1) If the court as a matter of law finds the contract or any clause of the contract to have been unconscionable at the time it was made the court may refuse to enforce the contract, or it may enforce the remainder of the contract without the unconscionable clause, or it may so limit the application of any unconscionable clause as to avoid any unconscionable result. (2) When it is claimed or appears to the court that the contract or any clause thereof may be unconscionable the parties shall be afforded a reasonable opportunity to present evidence as to its commercial setting, purpose and effect to aid the court in making the determination. 5A Del. C. 1953, Г‚В§Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚2-302;Г‚ 55 Del. Laws, c. 349. ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 2-303. Allocation or division of risks. Where this Article allocates a risk or a burden as between the parties ГўВЂВњunless otherwise agreedГўВЂВќ, the agreement may not only shift the allocation but may also divide the risk or burden. 5A Del. C. 1953, Г‚В§Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚2-303;Г‚ 55 Del. Laws, c. 349. ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 2-304. Price payable in money, goods, realty, or otherwise. (1) The price can be made payable in money or otherwise. perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates a 30-day notice for termination, whereas the changed contract reduces this to 10 days. The Perturbation Explanation suggests this might violate Delaware law, potentially requiring a 30-day notice for contract terminations in specific circumstances. The Law Explanation, referencing 6 Del. C. В§ 2-309(3) (though not provided in the snippets), supports the idea that a 10-day notice might be deemed unreasonable, particularly concerning capital maintenance for an insurance company. While the provided snippet from Title 6 doesn't explicitly state a 30-day requirement, it covers general obligations and construction of contract which are pertinent to notice periods. Given the explanations and the nature of the change, the reduced notice period creates a significant risk of non-compliance with Delaware law regarding reasonable notification for contract termination, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent not terminated previously by AIG pursuant to the foregoing, this Agreement will terminate automatically one year after the closing of any sale of the Company by AIG, and all provisions hereof will be of no further force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent not terminated previously by AIG pursuant to the foregoing, this Agreement will terminate automatically six months after the closing of any sale of the Company by AIG, and all provisions hereof will be of no further force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the automatic termination period from one year to six months after the sale of the company could violate the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing under Delaware law. This is because a shorter period might not allow sufficient time for the transition of capital maintenance responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Delaware law regarding implied duty of good faith and fair dealing in contracts.
perturbation.law_citation: Delaware jurisprudence regarding implied duty of good faith and fair dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract. Reducing the termination period could be seen as a violation if it deprives the Company of the benefits of the agreement without a fair reason, especially concerning capital maintenance, potentially affecting the company's financial stability after the sale.

perturbation.justification: The change from one year to six months for automatic termination after the sale of the company creates a legal contradiction because it may violate the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing. The perturbation explanation and law explanation both highlight that a shorter period might not allow sufficient time for the transition of capital maintenance responsibilities. This violates Delaware law because it deprives the Company of the benefits of the agreement without a fair reason, potentially affecting the company's financial stability after the sale.

perturbation.original_text: American International Group, Inc. 180 Maiden Lane New York, New York 10038 Attention: Secretary
perturbation.changed_text: American International Group, Inc. 180 Maiden Lane New York, New York 10038 Attention: No One
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specific contact (Secretary) for sending notices might violate the principle of providing adequate notice, as recognized under Delaware contract law. If AIG no longer employs a 'Secretary', the changed text should reflect a valid role, not a deliberately obstructive designation.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Delaware law regarding sufficient notice in contract law.
perturbation.law_citation: Delaware contract law principle of sufficient notice.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware contract law requires that notice provisions be reasonable and provide a means for the receiving party to actually receive the notice. Designating 'No One' as the recipient effectively prevents notice from being properly delivered, potentially invalidating any actions that depend on that notice.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 6 > Article 1 Authenticated PDF Г‚В§ 1-101 Г‚В§ 1-102 Г‚В§ 1-103 Г‚В§ 1-104 Г‚В§ 1-105 Г‚В§ 1-106 Г‚В§ 1-107 Г‚В§ 1-108 TITLE 6 Commerce and Trade SUBTITLE I Uniform Commercial Code ARTICLE 1. General Provisions Part 1 General Provisions Г‚В§ 1-101. Short titles. (a) This subtitle may be cited as the Uniform Commercial Code. (b) This article may be cited as Uniform Commercial Code ГўВЂВ” General Provisions. 5A Del. C. 1953, Г‚В§Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1-101;Г‚ 55 Del. Laws, c. 349 ;Г‚ 74 Del. Laws, c. 332, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1 ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 1-102. Scope of article. This article applies to a transaction to the extent that it is governed by another article of the Uniform Commercial Code. 74 Del. Laws, c. 332, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1 ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 1-103. Construction of Uniform Commercial Code to promote its purposes and policies; applicability of supplemental principles of law. (a) The Uniform Commercial Code must be liberally construed and applied to promote its underlying purposes and policies, which are: (1) To simplify, clarify, and modernize the law governing commercial transactions; (2) To permit the continued expansion of commercial practices through custom, usage, and agreement of the parties; and (3) To make uniform the law among the various jurisdictions. (b) Unless displaced by the particular provisions of the Uniform Commercial Code, the principles of law and equity, including the law merchant and the law relative to capacity to contract, principal and agent, estoppel, fraud, misrepresentation, duress, coercion, mistake, bankruptcy, and other validating or invalidating cause supplement its provisions. 5A Del. C. 1953, Г‚В§Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1-102;Г‚ 55 Del. Laws, c. 349 ;Г‚ 74 Del. Laws, c. 332, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1 ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 1-104. Construction against implied repeal. The Uniform Commercial Code being a general act intended as a unified coverage of its subject matter, no part of it shall be deemed to be impliedly repealed by subsequent legislation if such construction can reasonably be avoided. 74 Del. Laws, c. 332, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1 ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 1-105. Severability. If any provision or clause of the Uniform Commercial Code or its application to any person or circumstance is held invalid, the invalidity does not affect other provisions or applications of the Uniform Commercial Code which can be given effect without the invalid provision or application, and to this end the provisions of the Uniform Commercial Code are severable. 74 Del. Laws, c. 332, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1 ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 1-106. Use of singular and plural; gender. In the Uniform Commercial Code, unless the statutory context otherwise requires: (1) Words in the singular number include the plural, and those in the plural include the singular; and (2) Words of any gender also refer to any other gender. 74 Del. Laws, c. 332, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1 ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 1-107. Section captions. Section captions are part of the Uniform Commercial Code. 74 Del. Laws, c. 332, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1 ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 1-108. Relation to Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act. This subtitle modifies, limits, and supersedes the federal Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act, (15 U.S.C. perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the contact information from 'Secretary' to 'No One'. The Law Explanation states that Delaware law requires notice provisions to be reasonable and allow the receiving party to receive the notice, and that designating 'No One' prevents proper notice. Scraped Law Snippet 1 discusses the construction of the Uniform Commercial Code and supplemental principles of law. While it does not directly address notice requirements, the Law Explanation indicates that the modification contradicts the principle of reasonable notice under Delaware contract law, which is a supplemental principle of law. Therefore, the modified text likely introduces a legal contradiction.
